transcript.text: Hi, this is a video. Lying is bad. I just want to put it out there and try to say lying is bad. Don't lie.
I know there's lots of people out there who like to lie for fun, tell stories, just to, I guess, entertain people and to practice their own ability to make up stories and be creative.

But I think there's other ways to do that. You can tell stories in a funny way that's just fictional or something or say, oh, by the way, I was just lying because I don't think it's healthy for you in the long run if you're like that because then you just, you don't get the excitement out of telling somebody the truth, like a story that's true.
And then how are you going to believe anybody else's stories, like really, and experience the excitement of that because if you think that it could be a lie.

So, um, that kind of lying I think is bad and then lying for out of a personal gain I think is bad also, like, uh, but for those kinds of lies, those have to be, the causes of those lies have to be cut out.

So, um, like sometimes people have to lie, say that they've never smoked pot in their whole life and a job interviewer's application and that's kind of like being like, oh, you know, we want, we'll hire you, but are you, are you a liar?

See, so, so I don't, I don't think, I mean, I think like the whole war on drugs and like marijuana being illegal, that increases lies too because people have to lie about, um, that and then, um, because it's illegal, you get, um, underworld culture, gangster culture, gang, uh, criminal culture, and that's all about lying.

I think, um, I think, uh, like lying is one of the main reasons why we don't have, uh, UFO disclosure, I think, because people say stuff and they just think that they're lying, like witnesses, government witnesses, normal people, just can't tell anybody because in this culture so many people lie that, um, that, you know, we, for some reason we can't tell the difference.

When somebody is actually telling the truth. It's interesting how fused in and how much we, we actually are able to not believe somebody. I've told people stories and they've just been like this because, and I think maybe it's because I just thought I was lying.

But, uh, anyways, that's what I have to say about lying. I think it's bad. If you, if you, if you lie, you can't see, you can't see yourself. You can't feel, you don't know who you really are. You can't feel yourself. You don't know what makes you, you, and what makes everything about you unique.
If you're, if you're, if you're lying is kind of like the beginning of conforming, because you lie in order to say a certain thing, to be in a certain, uh, group, I guess.
Maybe you lie about stuff that you believe, and then you would do stuff, and then you would just kind of morph into like a cookie cutter type of situation, which isn't very fun.
I think part of being fun is, if we're brought about life, it's just being unique, doing your thing, and, uh, so you have to be honest, and not lie.
